vocabulary.name: State Department Consular Affairs Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain terms and definitions used in the US Department of State Consular Affairs Data API

terms.definition: An official US Department of State notice warning American citizens of safety and security conditions in foreign countries. Advisories are issued at four alert levels.
The numeric risk classification assigned to a country travel advisory, ranging from 1 to 4.

values.label: Exercise Normal Precautions
values.description: The lowest advisory level indicating normal safety and security concerns.

values.label: Exercise Increased Caution
values.description: Warns travelers to be aware of heightened risks such as political unrest or elevated crime rates.

values.label: Reconsider Travel
values.description: Advises travelers to reconsider travel due to serious risks to safety and security.

values.label: Do Not Travel
values.description: The highest advisory level recommending US citizens do not travel to the country due to life-threatening risks.

terms.definition: The bureau within the US Department of State responsible for protecting US citizens abroad, issuing passports and visas, and providing emergency assistance to Americans in foreign countries.
The Consular Affairs Data API, the public API platform hosted at cadataapi.state.gov that provides open, unauthenticated access to travel advisories, country information, and passport facility data.

A location authorized by the US Department of State to accept passport applications on behalf of the federal government. Includes USPS post offices, courthouses, public libraries, and municipal government offices.
A single-letter code identifying the type of passport acceptance facility.
A two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code used to identify countries in the API (e.g., 'AF' for Afghanistan, 'FR' for France).

Foreign Relations of the United States — a series of historical diplomatic records published by the State Department documenting US foreign policy decisions and negotiations. Available through a separate catalog API.
aliases: Foreign Relations of the United States

terms.definition: A country, territory, or geopolitical region as recognized by the US Department of State for travel advisory and information purposes.
The official passport, visa, and documentation requirements for entering and departing a country as established by that country's government and communicated by the US Department of State.
Exercise Normal Precautions — the lowest advisory level, indicating normal safety and security risks comparable to travel within the United States.
Exercise Increased Caution — advises travelers to be more attentive to their personal security due to heightened risks.
Reconsider Travel — advises US citizens to reconsider travel to the country due to serious safety and security threats.
Do Not Travel — the highest level, issued when life-threatening risks exist and the US government has limited ability to assist American citizens.
